name: Lester Young
abstract: Lester Willis Young (August 27, 1909 – March 15, 1959), nicknamed "Pres" or "Prez" (short for the "President of the tenor saxophone"), was an American jazz tenor saxophonist and occasional clarinetist. Coming to prominence while a member of Count Basie's orchestra, Young was one of the most influential players on his instrument.

about_quotes.speaker: Marty Grosz
about_quotes.evidence: I remember my first jazz record I ever bought for myself was a Commodore Record called “Paging the Devil” ... by Lester Young, Buck Clayton, and the Kansas City Six I think it was.
about_quotes.doc_title: Marty Grosz interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 4, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Freeman
about_quotes.evidence: You listen to the sound between a Coleman Hawkins and a Lester Young or Ben Webster and Lester Young, you can hear that Lester Young was completely about being his own man.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Freeman interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Jimmy Owens
about_quotes.evidence: Just listen to Coleman Hawkins, or Lester Young, or John Coltrane, or Johnny Hodges, or Charlie Shavers; listen to these people feel what they were dealing with
about_quotes.doc_title: Jimmy Owens — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Gary Burton
about_quotes.evidence: He’s played with Pat, he’s played with Chick, with me, with Charlie Parker, with Lester Young, with John Coltrane, all kinds of people, all through the years
about_quotes.doc_title: Gary Burton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Art Farmer
about_quotes.evidence: he started using me. That was great. I came in late a couple times... he said, 'You know Prez, the man is getting on me and I don’t want to hear nothing.'
about_quotes.doc_title: Art Farmer — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sherrie Maricle
about_quotes.evidence: You’d know instantly if it was Louis Armstrong or Clark Terry or the difference between Dexter Gordon or Lester Young or whatever
about_quotes.doc_title: Sherrie Maricle interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clearwater Beach, Florida, March 18, 2001 [interview 1 of 2]
